metric_name: Moderated 'Future of DVD' panel at DVD Forum 2003 US Conference
metric_value: Nov 21 2003, Beverly Hills CA: Panel speaker: Future of DVD (Panelists: Peter Kastner - Aberdeen Group [moderator], Brian Heuckroth - Cirrus Logic, Matt Jarman - ClearPlay)
notes: Kastner moderator role documented in Jim Taylor publications list

metric_name: DVD Forum 2003 US Conference venue and date

metric_value: Global DVD sell-through peaked 2006 at approximately $23B; Blu-ray launched 2006 failed to replace sales decline
notes: Captures the 2003 panel's immediate post-event reality

metric_name: HD DVD abandoned; Blu-ray wins format war
metric_value: Toshiba discontinued HD DVD 2008-02-19; Warner's Jan 2008 Blu-ray-exclusive announcement precipitated collapse
notes: Format war resolved ~5 years after Kastner's panel

metric_value: Netflix Watch Instantly launched Jan 2007; disrupted physical-media rental business and accelerated DVD decline
notes: Major disruption 3.5 years after Kastner's 2003 panel

metric_name: Family Movie Act 2005 legalized ClearPlay filtering
metric_value: Family Entertainment and Copyright Act 2005 included the Family Movie Act providing legal safe harbor for third-party content-filtering technology; passed Apr 27 2005
notes: Validated ClearPlay business model 18 months after panel

metric_name: DVD+Blu-ray US sales declined to niche
metric_value: US DVD+Blu-ray combined physical-media revenue fell to ~$1.5B (2023) from peak ~$16B (2006); category now <5% of video revenue vs streaming
